enrichment.short_description: Open-source, self-hostable task manager with list, Kanban, Gantt, and table views.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vikunja: open-source task manager. Self-host free (AGPLv3) or use Vikunja Cloud from €4/month with a 14-day trial.

features.title: Multiple views
features.description: Organize work using List, Kanban, Gantt, or Table views for the same project.

features.title: Self-hosting
features.description: Fully open-source (AGPLv3) and free to self-host, giving teams control over their own data.

features.title: Vikunja Cloud
features.description: A paid managed hosting option that handles updates and backups automatically.

features.title: Task details
features.description: Tasks support due dates, labels, descriptions, and file attachments.

features.title: Quick command search
features.description: A command-style search interface for fast navigation between tasks and projects.

features.title: Import tools
features.description: Built-in migration from Todoist, Trello, and Microsoft To Do.

features.title: Sharing and collaboration
features.description: Projects can be shared with others, with tasks assignable to team members.

pros: Open source (AGPLv3) and free to self-host indefinitely
Multiple project views (List, Kanban, Gantt, Table) in one tool
Built-in importers from Todoist, Trello, and Microsoft To Do
Vikunja Cloud includes a 14-day free trial and cancel-anytime billing
Independent, bootstrapped project not backed by VC funding
cons: Self-hosting requires running and maintaining your own server
Cloud plans are paid — no permanently free hosted tier
Attachment storage is capped by plan (10GB on Personal, 50GB on Organization)

faqs.question: Is Vikunja free?
faqs.answer: Yes, if self-hosted — Vikunja is open source under AGPLv3. The managed Vikunja Cloud service is paid, starting at €4/month.

faqs.question: Is Vikunja open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, its source code is publicly available under the AGPLv3 license.

faqs.question: Can I migrate my tasks from another app?
faqs.answer: Vikunja includes built-in import tools for Todoist, Trello, and Microsoft To Do.

faqs.question: Does Vikunja Cloud offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Vikunja Cloud plans include a 14-day free trial and can be cancelled anytime.

faqs.question: Is there a plan for teams?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Organization plan is priced per user (€5/month, or €50/year with an annual discount) and supports unlimited teams.

use_cases.title: Privacy-focused personal task management
use_cases.description: Individuals who want to own their task data can self-host Vikunja or use the Personal cloud plan.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted team project tracking
use_cases.description: Organizations that prefer running their own infrastructure can self-host Vikunja under its open-source license.

use_cases.title: Household task sharing
use_cases.description: The Family plan supports up to 5 users sharing projects under a single subscription.

features: Unlimited lists, tasks, reminders, filters, and share links

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Vikunja?
paragraphs: Vikunja is an open-source task and project management application that can be self-hosted or used through its managed Vikunja Cloud service.
It offers List, Kanban, Gantt, and Table views for the same underlying tasks, along with built-in importers for Todoist, Trello, and Microsoft To Do.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Vikunja is for
paragraphs: Vikunja suits individuals and organizations that want data ownership and are comfortable self-hosting an AGPLv3 open-source tool, as well as those who prefer a paid, managed cloud alternative with a 14-day trial.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source Kanban board software for self-hosted project and task management.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kanboard is a free, MIT-licensed, self-hosted Kanban board tool with WIP limits, automation, and LDAP/OAuth2 login.

features.title: Kanban board visualization
features.description: Drag-and-drop tasks between customizable columns to track project status.

features.title: Work-in-progress limits
features.description: Limit how many tasks can sit in a column at once to keep teams focused.

features.title: Subtasks, attachments, and comments
features.description: Tasks support subtasks, file attachments, and threaded comments.

features.title: Search and filter query language
features.description: A dedicated query syntax for searching and filtering tasks.

features.title: Automated actions
features.description: Trigger automated workflow actions based on task events.

features.title: LDAP and OAuth2 authentication
features.description: Supports LDAP and OAuth2 login providers for team access.

features.title: Multi-language interface
features.description: Translated into 30+ languages.

pros: Completely free under the permissive MIT license
Lightweight with a simple self-hosted installation
Large open-source contributor base (334+ contributors)
Automated workflow actions based on task events
LDAP and OAuth2 support for enterprise authentication
cons: No official hosted/cloud version — self-hosting only
Requires ongoing server maintenance and updates by the user
No AI features
Simpler, more minimal interface than many modern SaaS competitors

faqs.question: Is Kanboard free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kanboard is free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: Does Kanboard offer a hosted/cloud version?
faqs.answer: Kanboard is designed for self-hosted deployment; no official paid cloud hosting is offered on kanboard.org.

faqs.question: Can I use single sign-on with Kanboard?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kanboard supports LDAP and OAuth2 authentication providers.

faqs.question: Is Kanboard open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is distributed under the MIT License with contributions from 334+ people.

use_cases.title: Lightweight self-hosted Kanban
use_cases.description: Small teams that want a simple, self-hosted Kanban board without SaaS subscription costs.

use_cases.title: On-premises project tracking
use_cases.description: Organizations requiring full control over where task data is stored can run Kanboard on their own servers.

use_cases.title: Workflow automation via query language
use_cases.description: Teams that want to script filters and automated actions using Kanboard's built-in query syntax.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Kanboard?
paragraphs: Kanboard is a free, open-source Kanban board application built for self-hosted deployment. It emphasizes simplicity and visual clarity, letting teams organize tasks into customizable columns with drag-and-drop.
It is distributed under the MIT license and maintained by Frédéric Guillot along with more than 334 contributors.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Kanboard is for
paragraphs: Kanboard fits teams and individuals who want a free, self-hosted Kanban tool and are comfortable installing and maintaining it on their own infrastructure, including those who need LDAP or OAuth2 authentication.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, self-hosted Kanban board built with Meteor and MongoDB under the MIT license.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: WeKan is a free, open-source Kanban board with Docker, Snap, and Sandstorm deployment options and 105+ languages.

features.title: Real-time Kanban boards
features.description: Boards update in real time as collaborators make changes.

features.title: Customizable cards and lists
features.description: Boards, lists, and cards can be configured to fit different workflows.

features.title: Multiple deployment options
features.description: Deployable via Docker, Snap, Sandstorm, or direct source installation.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Translated into 105+ languages.

features.title: Active open development
features.description: Over 15,000 commits with 21,000+ GitHub stars and frequent updates.

features.title: Optional commercial support
features.description: Paid commercial support is available separately via wekan.fi.

pros: Fully free and open source under the MIT license
Very active development with frequent updates
Multiple deployment options (Docker, Snap, Sandstorm, source)
Large, active community (21,000+ GitHub stars, 3,000+ forks)
Translated into 105+ languages
cons: No official free-hosted SaaS version — requires self-hosting
Runs on Node.js, Meteor, and MongoDB, requiring technical setup
4GB RAM recommended for production use
Commercial support is a separate paid add-on

faqs.question: Is WeKan free?
faqs.answer: Yes, WeKan is free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: How is WeKan deployed?
faqs.answer: It can be self-hosted via Docker, Snap packages, Sandstorm, or installed directly from source.

faqs.question: Is commercial support available?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid commercial support is offered separately through wekan.fi.

faqs.question: What are the system requirements?
faqs.answer: A minimum of 1GB RAM is required, with 4GB recommended for production deployments; it uses Node.js, Meteor, and MongoDB.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted team Kanban
use_cases.description: Teams wanting a free, self-hosted alternative to commercial Kanban tools.

use_cases.title: Personal task boards
use_cases.description: Individuals organizing personal projects visually with kanban-style cards.

use_cases.title: Multi-language deployments
use_cases.description: Organizations needing a Kanban tool localized into one of 105+ supported languages.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Wekan?
paragraphs: WeKan is a free, open-source Kanban board application that can be self-hosted using Docker, Snap, Sandstorm, or a direct source install.
The project is under active development, built on Node.js, Meteor, and MongoDB, and has accumulated over 15,000 commits and 21,000+ GitHub stars.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Wekan is for
paragraphs: Wekan suits teams and individuals who want a free, actively-maintained, self-hosted Kanban board and are prepared to run their own Node.js/MongoDB infrastructure.

name: Focalboard
skip_reason: The official site (focalboard.com) returned an HTTP 530 server error and could not be reached. Independent research confirms the standalone open-source project has been unmaintained since it was split out in August 2024 (the team is seeking new maintainers), and Mattermost stopped officially supporting/bundling it as of September 2023 — the product is effectively orphaned.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, all-in-one platform combining project management, chat, docs, and video calls.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Huly is an open-source, all-in-one work platform with a free unlimited-user plan and paid tiers from $19.99/month.

features.title: Team Planner
features.description: A centralized calendar showing individual tasks across the team.

features.title: Project management with GitHub sync
features.description: Track tasks and sync issues with GitHub repositories.

features.title: Virtual Office
features.description: Video and audio conferencing with customizable virtual spaces.

features.title: Chat
features.description: Direct messaging and group conversations built into the platform.

features.title: Collaborative documents
features.description: Real-time collaborative document editing with rich formatting.

features.title: Unified inbox
features.description: Centralized notification management across the platform.

features.title: Open source
features.description: Source code is publicly available on GitHub.

pros: Free Common plan with unlimited users and 10GB storage
Open source and self-hostable via GitHub
Combines project management, chat, docs, and video in one platform
GitHub sync for project tracking
Usage-based paid tiers scale storage and video/audio traffic
cons: AI features (Huly MetaBrain) are announced but not yet available
Paid tiers are priced around storage/traffic caps rather than feature gates, which can be less predictable
Newer platform with a smaller ecosystem than established competitors like Jira or Notion

faqs.question: Is Huly free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Common plan is free with unlimited users, unlimited Huly objects, 10GB storage, and 10GB video/audio traffic.

faqs.question: What do the paid Huly plans cost?
faqs.answer: Rare is $19.99/month, Epic is $99.99/month, and Legendary is $399.99/month, each increasing storage and video/audio traffic allowances; custom pricing is also available.

faqs.question: Is Huly open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, its code is available on GitHub at hcengineering/platform.

faqs.question: Does Huly have AI features?
faqs.answer: AI capabilities (referred to as Huly MetaBrain) are announced but listed as still in development at the time of writing.

use_cases.title: Replacing multiple SaaS tools
use_cases.description: Teams wanting to consolidate Linear, Jira, Slack, and Notion-style tools into one platform.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted all-in-one workspace
use_cases.description: Organizations that want to self-host an open-source alternative to proprietary work platforms.

use_cases.title: GitHub-integrated project tracking
use_cases.description: Development teams that want project tasks synced directly with GitHub.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Huly?
paragraphs: Huly is an open-source, all-in-one platform that bundles project management, team chat, collaborative documents, and video conferencing into a single workspace.
It offers a free Common plan with unlimited users, and paid tiers (Rare, Epic, Legendary) that scale storage and video/audio traffic allowances.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Huly is for
paragraphs: Huly suits teams looking to replace several separate tools (project tracking, chat, docs, video) with one platform, including those who want an open-source, self-hostable option.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source Application Lifecycle Management platform for agile planning, requirements, and testing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tuleap is an open-source ALM platform for agile project management, requirements traceability, and test management.

features.title: Agile project management
features.description: Supports Scrum, Kanban, and hybrid agile frameworks.

features.title: Requirements management
features.description: Baseline tracking and requirements traceability.

features.title: Test management
features.description: Centralized test planning and validation.

features.title: SAFe program management
features.description: Support for scaled agile (SAFe) at the enterprise level.

features.title: Issue tracking and DevOps
features.description: Integrated Git repositories and CI/CD capabilities.

features.title: Compliance and traceability
features.description: Supports ISO, ASPICE, and IEC 62304 compliance requirements.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Available as cloud, on-premises, or air-gapped deployments, including France-based hosting for data sovereignty.

pros: Open-source, auditable core with no proprietary black box
Strong compliance support (ISO, ASPICE, IEC 62304) for regulated industries
Flexible deployment: cloud, on-premises, or air-gapped
Covers the full ALM lifecycle in one platform (planning, requirements, testing, DevOps)
Tuleap Campus offers a free option for educational/community use
cons: Enterprise pricing is not publicly listed
Broad feature set may be more complex than needed for small teams
Now part of the Dassault Systèmes portfolio, which may shift product direction over time

faqs.question: Is Tuleap open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tuleap has an open-source, auditable core.

faqs.question: What is Tuleap Campus?
faqs.answer: A free offering intended for educational and community use.

faqs.question: Can Tuleap be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports on-premises and air-gapped deployments in addition to cloud hosting.

faqs.question: Who develops Tuleap?
faqs.answer: Tuleap is developed by Enalean SAS, a European software company, and the product has joined the Dassault Systèmes portfolio.

use_cases.title: Regulated industry compliance
use_cases.description: Teams needing ISO, ASPICE, or IEC 62304 traceability for medical, automotive, or aerospace software.
use_cases.audience: Regulated engineering and medical device teams

use_cases.title: Open-source ALM for enterprises
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting an auditable, open-source alternative to proprietary ALM suites.

use_cases.title: Scaled agile program management
use_cases.description: Enterprises running SAFe programs across multiple teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tuleap?
paragraphs: Tuleap is an open-source Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) platform that centralizes agile project management, requirements traceability, test management, and DevOps in one tool.
It is developed by Enalean SAS and offers cloud, on-premises, and air-gapped deployment options, with a free Tuleap Campus tier for education and community use.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tuleap is for
paragraphs: Tuleap fits organizations in regulated industries needing compliance traceability, as well as enterprises wanting an open-source, self-hostable ALM platform covering the full development lifecycle.

name: Phabricator
skip_reason: The official site explicitly states Phabricator has not been actively maintained since June 1, 2021, when Phacility wound down operations. The product is discontinued and no longer receiving updates.

enrichment.short_description: JetBrains' project management and issue tracker, free for teams of up to 10 users.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: YouTrack by JetBrains is a free issue tracker for teams up to 10 users, with paid Cloud plans from $4.50/user/month.

features.title: Free for small teams
features.description: Free for teams of up to 10 users.

features.title: Agile boards
features.description: Board-based views for tracking issues and project management.

features.title: Helpdesk projects
features.description: Free for up to 3 support agents with unlimited reporters.

features.title: Cloud and self-hosted options
features.description: Available as JetBrains-hosted Cloud or a self-managed Server/on-prem deployment.

features.title: Free trial
features.description: A free trial is available for evaluating paid tiers.

features.title: JetBrains ecosystem integration
features.description: Built by JetBrains, the company behind IntelliJ IDEA and other developer tools.

pros: Completely free for teams of up to 10 users
Helpdesk projects free for up to 3 agents with unlimited reporters
Per-user Cloud pricing decreases at higher user volumes
Choice of Cloud or self-hosted deployment
Backed by JetBrains, an established developer tools company
cons: JetBrains raised Cloud per-user prices effective October 2025
Per-user pricing model can become costly for larger teams (11+ users)
Detailed self-hosted/Server pricing was not confirmed on the pages reviewed

faqs.question: Is YouTrack free?
faqs.answer: Yes, YouTrack Cloud is free for teams of up to 10 users, and Helpdesk projects are free for up to 3 agents.

faqs.question: How much does YouTrack cost beyond 10 users?
faqs.answer: As of October 2025 pricing, Cloud subscriptions start around $5.40/user/month billed monthly or $4.50/user/month billed annually, with per-user rates decreasing as more users are added.

faqs.question: Does YouTrack offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial is available via the product's download/trial page.

faqs.question: Who makes YouTrack?
faqs.answer: YouTrack is developed by JetBrains.

use_cases.title: Free issue tracking for small teams
use_cases.description: Teams of up to 10 users can use YouTrack Cloud at no cost.

use_cases.title: Developer-centric project management
use_cases.description: Teams already using JetBrains IDEs who want an integrated issue tracker.

use_cases.title: Customer support ticketing
use_cases.description: Support teams can use YouTrack's Helpdesk projects, free for up to 3 agents.

features: Helpdesk free for up to 3 agents, unlimited reporters
Per-user rate decreases at higher volumes

long_form_blocks.heading: What is YouTrack?
paragraphs: YouTrack is a project management and issue tracking tool made by JetBrains. It is free for teams of up to 10 users and offers paid Cloud plans beyond that, with per-user pricing that decreases at scale.
It also includes Helpdesk-style support projects, free for up to 3 agents with unlimited reporters, and can be deployed via JetBrains' Cloud or as a self-hosted instance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who YouTrack is for
paragraphs: YouTrack suits small teams that want a free issue tracker, as well as larger development and support organizations, particularly those already using other JetBrains developer tools.

enrichment.short_description: Product management suite for roadmapping, strategy, ideas, and delivery, with a 30-day free trial.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Aha! is a product management suite for roadmaps, strategy, and delivery, starting at $74.59/user/month with a free trial.

features.title: Aha! Roadmaps
features.description: Strategic planning and roadmap visualization.

features.title: Aha! Discovery
features.description: Customer interview management for product research.

features.title: Aha! Ideas
features.description: Feedback collection and idea consolidation.

features.title: Aha! Whiteboards
features.description: Collaborative ideation and wireframing.

features.title: Aha! Builder
features.description: AI-powered prototype and application creation.

features.title: Aha! Develop and Teamwork
features.description: Engineering workflow management and project delivery coordination.

features.title: Elle AI assistant
features.description: An AI assistant built into the platform to accelerate product work.

features.title: Free read-only users
features.description: On Enterprise and Enterprise+ plans, reviewer and viewer seats are free.

pros: Comprehensive suite covering strategy through delivery in one platform
Free reviewer/viewer seats on Enterprise and Enterprise+ plans
Built-in Elle AI assistant and AI-powered Aha! Builder
40+ tool integrations included even on the entry Premium plan
30-day free trial to evaluate before purchasing
cons: No public free plan — paid only, starting at $74.59/user/month
Many capabilities (Discovery, Builder, Develop, Teamwork, Knowledge Advanced) are priced as separate add-ons
Enterprise+ requires annual billing
Premium pricing tier makes it a bigger investment than lighter product-management tools

faqs.question: Does Aha! offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: No public free plan is offered; Aha! provides a 30-day free trial instead.

faqs.question: What is the starting price for Aha!?
faqs.answer: The Premium plan starts at $74.59 per user per month.

faqs.question: Are viewer and reviewer seats free?
faqs.answer: Yes, on the Enterprise and Enterprise+ plans, read-only users (reviewers and viewers) are free.

faqs.question: Does Aha! have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes the Elle AI assistant and Aha! Builder, an AI-powered prototype and application creation tool.

faqs.question: Can I add other Aha! products individually?
faqs.answer: Yes, products like Aha! Discovery, Develop, Teamwork, and Builder are available as separate add-ons with their own per-user pricing.

use_cases.title: End-to-end product management
use_cases.description: Product teams wanting one suite spanning strategy, roadmapping, ideas, and delivery tracking.

use_cases.title: Enterprise stakeholder visibility
use_cases.description: Organizations that need many read-only stakeholders to view roadmaps without paying per seat.
use_cases.audience: Enterprise organizations with large stakeholder groups

use_cases.title: AI-assisted prototyping
use_cases.description: Teams that want to generate prototypes and applications with Aha! Builder's AI capabilities.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Aha!?
paragraphs: Aha! is a product management platform made up of several integrated products — Roadmaps, Discovery, Ideas, Whiteboards, Builder, Develop, Teamwork, and Knowledge — covering the full cycle from strategy to delivery.
Plans start at $74.59 per user per month on the Premium tier, with higher Enterprise tiers offering free read-only seats and additional capabilities like OKRs and capacity planning.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Aha! is for
paragraphs: Aha! suits product teams and larger enterprises that want a single, comprehensive suite for roadmapping, feedback, and delivery, especially organizations with many stakeholders who only need read-only visibility.

enrichment.short_description: Product management platform for centralizing feedback, roadmapping, and AI-assisted spec generation.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Productboard centralizes customer feedback and roadmaps, with a free plan and paid tiers from $19/maker/month.

features.title: Customer insights
features.description: Centralize feedback from multiple sources and identify trends.

features.title: Roadmapping
features.description: Align teams on priorities and communicate product direction.

features.title: AI spec generation (Spark)
features.description: Convert ideas into delivery-ready specifications using built-in AI agents.

features.title: Strategic planning
features.description: Connect feature ideas to business objectives and goals.

features.title: Cross-functional collaboration
features.description: Shared workspace for product managers, developers, leadership, marketing, and sales.

features.title: Impact measurement
features.description: Track outcomes from specs through shipped features.

features.title: Product usage integrations
features.description: Connects with tools like Amplitude and Mixpanel on paid plans.

pros: Free plan available with no credit card required
Spark AI features included across all plans, with monthly AI credits
Business plan includes a 14-day free trial
Used by large companies including Autodesk, Zoom, Salesforce, and Coca-Cola
Enterprise plan adds SAML SSO and SCIM provisioning
cons: AI usage is capped by monthly credits, with paid top-ups required beyond that
Business plan requires a minimum of 2 makers; Enterprise requires a minimum of 5
Free plan is limited to 500 feedback notes, 25 contributors, and 1 teamspace
Business/Enterprise per-maker pricing rises quickly compared to the Free/Plus tiers

faqs.question: Does Productboard have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes unlimited roadmaps, 500 feedback notes, 25 contributors, and 1 teamspace at $0/month.

faqs.question: What is the starting paid price?
faqs.answer: The Plus plan starts at $19/maker/month billed annually, or $25/maker/month billed monthly.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Business plan offers a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: Does Productboard include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, all plans include Spark AI features with a monthly AI credit allowance that scales by plan.

faqs.question: What does the Enterprise plan add?
faqs.answer: Enterprise adds unlimited contributors, SAML SSO, SCIM provisioning, custom roles/permissions, and a Salesforce integration, with a minimum of 5 makers.

use_cases.title: Centralizing customer feedback
use_cases.description: Product teams that need to aggregate feedback from many channels into one place.

use_cases.title: Free entry-level roadmapping
use_cases.description: Small teams or startups that want a no-cost roadmap tool before upgrading.

use_cases.title: Enterprise product operations
use_cases.description: Larger organizations needing SSO, SCIM, and custom permissions for product management at scale.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Productboard?
paragraphs: Productboard is a product management platform that centralizes customer feedback, helps teams build and align on roadmaps, and uses built-in AI (Spark) to turn ideas into delivery-ready specifications.
It offers a free plan alongside paid Plus, Business, and Enterprise tiers priced per 'maker' (active product team member), with AI credit allowances that scale by plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Productboard is for
paragraphs: Productboard fits product teams of any size — from startups using the free plan to enterprise product organizations that need SSO, SCIM, and custom permissions on the Enterprise tier.
